title: lavandula angustifolia var. pyrenaica
intro: Lavandula angustifolia var. pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean lavender, is a subspecies of the narrow-leaved lavender plant, native to the Pyrenees mountains. This plant is prized for its calming and soothing properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate. The medicinal properties of Lavandula angustifolia var. pyrenaica include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations such as teas, oils, and infusions can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and soothing digestive issues.
